Title: Acoustic Cue Alignment in Audio Language Models for Speech Emotion Recognition
Affiliation: University of Augsburg, Technical University of Munich, Imperial College London
Author: Iosif Tsangko, Andreas Triantafyllopoulos, Björn W. Schuller
Description: A probing study testing whether instruction-following audio language models use explicit acoustic concept tokens (six interpretable cues derived from the eGeMAPS feature set: energy, pitch, dynamics, brightness, formants, and voice quality) in a grounded way for speech emotion recognition. On FAU-Aibo and IEMOCAP, aligned tokens improve unweighted average recall while shuffled, conflicting, or corrupted tokens degrade it.
